senses.definition: to intensify a disagreement or argument

examples.x: He is adding fuel to the fire by insisting on his point of view.
If you confront him, he will add fuel to the fire by getting defensive.
She always adds fuel to the fire by gossiping about others.
The manager added fuel to the fire by announcing layoffs during the meeting.
They have added fuel to the fire by ignoring the pleas for help.

senses.definition: to worsen a bad situation by doing or saying something hurtful or disrespectful, in addition to the harm that has already been caused

examples.x: He always adds insult to injury by criticizing her in public.
If you refuse to listen to her, you will be adding insult to injury by making her feel unheard.
She is adding insult to injury by laughing at him while he's down.
The company added insult to injury by not compensating the employees for their overtime work.
They have added insult to injury by refusing to apologize for their mistake.

senses.definition: to worsen a bad, unpleasant, or dangerous situation for someone or something

examples.x: He always fans the flames of controversy by making provocative statements.
If you keep criticizing her, you will be fanning the flames of her anger.
She is fanning the flames of the argument by not admitting her mistake.
The politician fanned the flames of nationalism by making a speech that appealed to people's emotions.
They have fanned the flames of discontent by ignoring the complaints of their customers.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˌoʊ tə hˈɛl ɪn ɐ hˈændbæskɪt hˈændbæɡ

senses.definition: to rapidly and irreversibly deteriorate or decline in a manner that is likely to result in a bad outcome or failure

examples.x: She is going to hell in handbag by spending all her money on unnecessary things.
The company is going to hell in a handbasket because of their poor management.
The project went to hell in a handbag because of the team's lack of coordination.
They have gone to hell in handbasket by ignoring the warning signs of the impending crisis.
If we don't take steps to improve the situation, our economy will go to the dogs.
The city's downtown area is going to the dogs because of the rising crime rate.
The company is going to the dogs by losing its market share to competitors.
The neighborhood went to the dogs when the factories closed and the jobs disappeared.
They have gone to the dogs by neglecting their health and well-being.

senses.definition: to be in a very poor condition, particularly compared to the past

examples.x: She is feeling tired and worn out, as if she has known better days.
The car had seen better days and broke down on the highway.
The old building has seen better days and is in need of repairs.
They have seen better days financially, but they are hopeful for the future.
This old car has seen better days, but it still runs.

senses.definition: to unintentionally do something that harms oneself or one's own interests

examples.x: He always shoots himself in the foot by speaking without thinking first.
If you don't double-check your work, you will end up shooting yourself in the foot.
She is shooting herself in the foot by missing all her deadlines.
The company shot itself in the foot by releasing a faulty product that damaged their reputation.
They have shot themselves in the foot by not preparing for the upcoming exam.

senses.definition: a situation in which one gets affected by two unpleasant or unfortunate events that happen at once

examples.x: He is facing a double whammy of health problems and financial difficulties.
If we don't prepare for the upcoming storm, we could face a double whammy of power outages and flooding.
She experienced a double whammy of losing her job and getting into a car accident.
The team faced a double whammy of injuries and bad weather during the championship game.
They have been hit with a double whammy of a natural disaster and an economic recession.

senses.definition: in a very poor condition or near the end of their lifespan or usefulness

examples.x: If we don't get help soon, our small business will be on its last legs.
She is on her last legs with exhaustion after working long hours all week.
The old car is on its last legs and needs to be replaced soon.
The old dog was on his last legs and had to be put down due to his failing health.
They have been on their last legs financially for months and are struggling to make ends meet.

senses.definition: very likely to be defeated or completely fail

examples.x: If we don't find a solution soon, our company will be on the ropes and may have to close down.
She is on the ropes with stress and anxiety from her heavy workload.
The boxer was on the ropes after being knocked down twice in the first round.
The team is on the ropes after losing their star player to injury.
They have been on the ropes financially since the pandemic began and are struggling to stay afloat.

word_list.word: out of the frying pan (and|) into the fire
word_list.pronunciation: ˌaʊɾəv ðə fɹˈaɪɪŋ pˈæn ænd ˌɪntʊ ðə fˈaɪɚ

senses.definition: used when one moves from a bad situation into a worse one

examples.x: He always seems to jump out of the frying pan and into the fire by making impulsive decisions.
She is going out of the frying pan and into the fire by quitting her stable job without a backup plan.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is really tired after an exhaustive physical activity

examples.x: If you don't take care of yourself, you will be feeling worse for wear in the morning.
She is feeling worse for wear after staying up all night to finish a project.
The car was looking worse for wear after being in a collision.
The old couch is looking worse for wear after years of use.
They have been through a lot and are looking worse for wear, but they are still determined to keep going.

senses.definition: used to indicate that a situation is about to become tense, difficult, or critical
to suddenly and completely stop, often in a dramatic or unexpected manner, implying a sense of frustration, disappointment, or failure

examples.x: If we don't address the issue soon, the entire system will come to a grinding halt.
She is worried that the negotiations will come to a grinding halt if the other party doesn't compromise.
The construction project came to a grinding halt due to a shortage of building materials.
The production line came to a grinding halt when the machinery broke down.
They have been working tirelessly, but the project has come to a grinding halt due to a lack of funding.

senses.definition: to slowly come to a complete stop after gradually losing speed or power

examples.x: She is worried that the project will grind to a halt if they don't receive the necessary support.
The traffic always seems to grind to a halt during rush hour.
They have been working hard, but the company's growth has ground to a halt in recent months.

senses.definition: to become ruined or destroyed, often due to a person's own actions or neglect

examples.x: If you don't take care of your health, it will go to the Devil.
She is worried that her relationship with her partner is going to the Devil.
They have been neglecting their responsibilities, and their business has gone to the Devil as a result.
